---
title: "Oublic History Media"
format: html
---

---
title: "Public History Media"
---

These videos represent my work in public history — translating academic 
research into accessible content for broader audiences. Each video draws 
on primary sources and scholarly literature to tell stories of Indigenous 
resistance in Virginia that are often left out of mainstream historical narratives.

---

## Bacon's Rebellion, Cockacoeske's Brilliance, and Howard Zinn

This video challenges Howard Zinn's telling of Bacon's Rebellion in *A People's 
History of the United States*. While Zinn frames the rebellion as a moment of 
interracial class solidarity, he omits the parallel story of Cockacoeske, 
the Pamunkey queen who leveraged the same political moment to secure the 
Treaty of Middle Plantation — a document that would serve as the foundation 
for state and federal recognition of Virginia's Indigenous peoples centuries later.

<iframe width="100%" height="415" 
src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/WZbsHdj6E08" 
title="Bacon's Rebellion, Cockacoeske's Brilliance and Howard Zinn"
allowfullscreen></iframe>

---

## The War on Eugenics: Indigenous Draft Resistance in World War II

Virginia's 1924 Racial Integrity Act reclassified Indigenous people as Black, 
stripping them of their identity through the pseudoscience of eugenics. When 
World War II began, Native men were assigned to Black units despite having 
served in white units in WWI. This video traces the concerted resistance 
effort — fought in courtrooms and in public opinion — that ultimately won.

<iframe width="100%" height="415" 
src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/YY_M5bAe9zs" 
title="The War on Eugenics"
allowfullscreen></iframe>

---

## Monacan School Integration in Amherst County

While events like the Little Rock Nine are well known, Virginia's Monacan 
people fought their own campaign to integrate historically white schools in 
Amherst County. This video tells that story — a local but deeply significant 
chapter in both Indigenous history and the broader history of civil rights 
in America.

<iframe width="100%" height="415" 
src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/Y5IXjY5WGs8" 
title="Monacan School Integration"
allowfullscreen></iframe>

---

## The Long Road to Recognition of the Pamunkey

This video connects the threads of resistance across these stories — from 
Cockacoeske's treaty to the draft resistance to school integration — tracing 
how each struggle contributed to the modern fight for federal recognition 
of Virginia's Indigenous peoples.

<iframe width="100%" height="415" 
src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/tj43PQVAfHI" 
title="The Long Road to Recognition of the Pamunkey"
allowfullscreen></iframe>

---

*These videos are also available on my [YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/@david_t_watkeys){target="_blank"}.*
